evals.prompt: install skill from https://github.com/vercel-labs/agent-skills/tree/main/skills/react-best-practices to my project
evals.expected_output: Claude parses the URL, downloads via git sparse-checkout, runs security assessment, shows in-chat gist + full report path, asks for install confirmation, identifies ./.opencode/skills/ as project-local destination.

assertions.text: Claude correctly parses the GitHub URL into owner, repo, branch, path, and skill_name (react-best-practices)
Claude uses git sparse-checkout (not a full clone) to download only the skill subdirectory
A security-report.md file is produced with a numeric score and at least one findings section
An in-chat security gist is shown with score, summary, and full report file path
Project-local destination is identified as ./.opencode/skills/react-best-practices/ (not machine-wide)
Claude asks the user to confirm before installing (does not auto-install)
Claude mentions cleaning up the temp directory after installation

evals.prompt: hey can you grab this skill and install it machine-wide? https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code-agent-skills/tree/main/skills/supabase-helper
evals.expected_output: Claude attempts git sparse-checkout, gets a 404 not found error, explains the error clearly, offers recovery options, confirms nothing was installed and temp was cleaned up.

assertions.text: Claude correctly parses the URL extracting owner=anthropics, repo=claude-code-agent-skills, skill_name=supabase-helper
Claude identifies machine-wide destination as ~/.config/opencode/skills/supabase-helper/
Claude attempts git sparse-checkout (or equivalent) to download the skill
When the repo is not found, Claude gives a clear error message explaining why (repo doesn't exist / may be private) rather than crashing silently
Claude confirms nothing was installed and temp files were cleaned up
Claude suggests actionable next steps (check URL, authenticate for private repos, try correct URL)

evals.prompt: install https://github.com/vercel-labs/agent-skills/tree/main/skills/react-best-practices — and make sure you do a thorough security check before installing
evals.expected_output: Claude downloads the skill, runs all 8 deterministic checks and 6 semantic checks, produces a full security-report.md, shows a concise in-chat gist with score and file path, then asks whether to install and where.

assertions.text: Security report covers all 8 deterministic check categories (shell patterns, exfiltration, secrets, file types, external URLs, filesystem writes, obfuscation, file size)
Security report covers all 6 LLM semantic categories (prompt injection, scope manipulation, social engineering, data exfiltration, privilege escalation, misleading identity)
A numeric score between 0-100 is computed and clearly displayed
The full report shows a score breakdown table explaining which deductions were applied and why
A full security-report.md file is saved and its path is given to the user in the chat
The in-chat gist is compact (not the full report) — shows score, brief summary, notable findings, and path to full report
After the security report, Claude asks whether to install and offers project-local vs machine-wide options
The report includes a plain-language recommendation section that a non-expert could understand
